children.content: Happy flow — Add CMS Context to Section
User selects a section, opens the Add Context modal, checks a CMS context (e.g., Articles), applies — and sees the context pill appear on the canvas section.
Section selected → Inspector → Context tab → "+ Add Context"

User selects a section on the canvas.
Inspector opens with Context tab visible.

Add Context modal appears showing
scope info and available contexts.

User checks "Articles" context.
Apply button activates with "+1" badge.

Context applied to section.
Modal closes, canvas updates.

Inspector Context tab now shows
Articles card with available fields.

name: Context Tab Flows — Flow 1: Happy Path

children.content: Shadowing — Override Page Context at Section
Adding a context at section level that already exists on the parent page creates a local override (shadow). The page instance is hidden for elements in this section.
Section inherits from page → Inspector → Context tab → "+ Add Context"

Page already has Articles context.
Section inherits it automatically.

Modal shows Articles with
"Available from page" badge.

Checking Articles shows orange warning:
"Overrides page instance."

Section now has its own Articles instance.
Page instance is hidden for this scope.
When viewing page scope, contexts that exist only on a child section show a "↑ Promote to page" action. This moves the context from the section to page level for broader access.
Page selected → Inspector → Context tab → "+ Add Context" → See section-only context

Page-level Add Context modal.
Articles exists only on child section.

User clicks "↑ Promote to page".
Context moves from section to page.

Articles now on page scope.
All sections can inherit and use it.

name: Context Tab Flows — Flow 3: Promote to Page

children.content: Users can check/uncheck multiple contexts before applying. All changes are committed at once. The Apply button shows a dirty state diff like "+2 −1".
Section with existing contexts → Inspector → "+ Add Context"

Modal opens showing Weather App
already attached (checked).

Uncheck Weather App. Check Articles
and My Team. Dirty state: "+2 −1"

Result: Weather App removed.
Articles and My Team now attached.
When no existing context matches, the user can create one directly from the modal: "+ New collection" under CMS, or "+ New custom context" under Custom group.
Add Context modal → "+ New collection" or "+ New custom context"

Modal open. User clicks
"+ New collection" link under CMS.

New collection created and
automatically checked in the list.

name: Context Tab Flows — Flow 5: Create New Context

children.content: If the user closes the modal (X button or clicks overlay) with pending unsaved changes, a "Discard changes?" confirmation dialog appears to prevent accidental loss.

User checks a context.
Apply button now active.

Confirmation dialog: "Discard changes?"
User chooses Discard or Cancel.

name: Context Tab Flows — Flow 6: Discard Changes

children.content: If a page context was previously removed from a section, the modal shows a "Restore page context" option with a dashed border, allowing re-inheritance.

Modal shows a dashed-border option
"Restore page context" for Articles.

Context restored. Section now
inherits Articles from page again.

name: Context Tab Flows — Flow 7: Restore Inherited

children.content: On dynamic pages, the page context is locked and cannot be removed. It appears with a lock icon and dimmed styling. This ensures the page's primary data source is always available.

Modal on a dynamic page.
Primary context is locked with 🔒 icon.

Primary context cannot be toggled.
It always remains available.

name: Context Tab Flows — Flow 8: Primary Lock

children.content: Repeater & Context — how the repeater connects to data and what happens at each step.
The repeater can be a context provider (context attached directly to it), or it can consume context from a parent (section/page).
The repeater's only data property. Binds to any array field from any context in scope.
When a list context is attached, "Items" auto-binds to its array field — unless already bound to another context.
The repeater is the list. The user connects it to data, and inner elements bind to fields from each item.
Contexts can attach directly to the repeater, but it can also consume context from a parent (section/page). Allows 2 repeaters with same context type but different configs.
When binding "Items" via the Connect panel, the repeater's own context is the default source. Parent contexts appear as alternatives.
"Items" auto-binds to the context's array field when a list context is attached — unless already bound to another context.
Coupled context → duplicated (new instance). Parent context → not duplicated (binding references parent). Paste out of context → disconnected.
Coupled context moves with repeater. Inherited context: TBD — user must choose: copy to new section / promote to page / disconnect.
Sort, filter, and page size are configured on the context instance, not the repeater itself. Settings apply only to the main records array.

"Items" can bind to any array field. Records array = main collection with sort/filter/page size. Item-level array = nested value per record (no config). Inner elements bind to item fields (per row) or context fields (same across rows).
Two states look identical on canvas (context attached but unbound vs. no context at all). Canvas indication must distinguish them so the user knows what action to take.
The repeater can have a context attached while "Items" is bound to a different context from a parent scope. The settings panel may need to highlight this mismatch clearly.
The following flows describe the key interactions when working with repeaters and context.
Drag from add panel. Matching context already on page → inherit it.
Drag from add panel. No matching context → context created on repeater.
Sort by title A/Z. Filter out if description is empty.
Promote context to section → external controls can bind to it.
Switch to a different context already on the page to feed the repeater.
Unbind repeater from data source. Confirmation dialog → all inner bindings removed.
Select context: from existing, or create a new one.
Show 2 repeaters with different configuration of the same context.
Flow 1 — Pre-Bound Repeater, Context Exists on Page
User drags a pre-bound repeater (e.g., Products Gallery) from the Add Panel onto a section. A matching context already exists on the page — the repeater inherits it.

Click repeater header to select it.
Inspector opens with Settings tab.

Inspector shows repeater settings.
Items connected to Articles collection.

Click a text element inside repeater item.
Inspector shows element bindings.

Title element shows bound field.
Data flows from repeater context.

name: Repeater Flows — Flow 1: Pre-Bound Repeater, Context Exists on Page

children.content: Unbind the repeater from its data source. Confirmation dialog warns about inner bindings being removed. Repeater returns to placeholder state.
User adds a blank repeater with no context attached. They connect it by selecting an existing context or creating a new one.

Blank Repeater section visible.
'Not connected' notice displayed.

Click repeater to select.
Inspector shows unconnected state.

Repeater settings inspector.
Items shows 'Connect to an array field'.

Click 'Connect data →' link.
Opens the Add Context modal.

Add Context modal shows data sources.
Select Articles from the list.

Articles checked. Click Apply changes.
Badge shows +1 context added.

Repeater now connected to Articles.
Inspector shows data attached.
